name: Njord
description: Njord is a Vanir god associated with the sea, seafaring, wind, fishing, wealth, and crop fertility. Unlike the Aesir, Njord and his children, Freyr and Freya, originate from the Vanir, a different race of gods known for their connection to nature and fertility. Njord's origins are shrouded in some mystery, but he came to live among the Aesir as part of a hostage exchange following the Aesir-Vanir War. As a god of the sea, Njord is believed to have power over the waves and winds, granting safe passage to sailors and ensuring bountiful catches for fishermen. He is also associated with wealth, as the sea was a crucial source of trade and prosperity in Norse society. Njord's connection to fertility extends to both the land and sea, influencing crop yields and the abundance of marine life. He resided in Nóatún ('ship enclosure'), a dwelling in Asgard. His marriage to the giantess Skaði was an unhappy one, as she preferred the mountains while he preferred the sea. Njord is a powerful and benevolent deity, revered for his control over the elements and his ability to bring prosperity and good fortune to those who seek his favor. He represents the bounty and power of the natural world, and the crucial role that the sea played in Norse life.
subtitle: God of the Sea, Wind, and Wealth

keyMyths.title: Njord's Marriage to Skaði
keyMyths.description: Njord's marriage to the giantess Skaði was a result of the Aesir-Vanir War. Their incompatibility due to their differing preferences for the sea and mountains led to their separation.

keyMyths.title: Njord's Origins
keyMyths.description: Njord's origins lie with the Vanir, a race of gods associated with nature and fertility. He came to live with the Aesir as part of a peace treaty after the Aesir-Vanir war.

keyMyths.title: Njord's Dwelling in Nóatún
keyMyths.description: Njord resides in Nóatún, meaning 'ship enclosure', a dwelling in Asgard associated with his connection to the sea. This is where he likely receives prayers and offerings from seafarers.

sources.author: Snorri Sturluson
sources.description: A key source for Norse mythology, providing detailed accounts of the gods and their stories.
